D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Memory Colour Shift — Remembered Bananas Are Yellower

Hansen 2006: a grey banana is remembered as slightly yellow because the brain biases ambiguous percepts toward stored object knowledge ("memory colour")

Memory-colour effects show top-down priors influence the percept. Equally grey banana vs lemon vs orange shapes shift toward their typical colour. Bias is strongest in achromatic test stimuli, and contributes to discounting of context illumination.

Slide pure-grey value to see remembered colour shift.
